title: Alisa Morss Clyne Named Interim Chair of UMD Bioengineering
excerpt: Alisa Morss Clyne will serve as interim chair of UMD Bioengineering starting Aug. 1, 2026.

title: BIOE Faculty Member Develops Low-Cost Cervical Pre-Cancer Treatment Device for Low and Middle-Income Countries
excerpt: Jenna Mueller leads new study on speculum-compatible injector technology for LMICs

title: Alisa Clyne Recognized for Teaching Excellence, Faculty Service
excerpt: Alisa Clyne was recognized for two faculty awards at the 2025 Faculty and Staff Recognition Celebration.

title: ElevateHER: Engineering Solutions for Women's Health
excerpt: Erika Moore co-chaired the NSF ElevateHER Conference to improve research in women's health.

title: Joe Huang Recieves Two NIH Funding Awards
excerpt: New Grants Totaling $6M to UMCP-UMB Team Support Research to Improve Brain and Ovarian Cancer Treatments

title: Fischell Fellow Spotlight: Alisa Clyne
excerpt: Clyne's area of expertise is cardiovascular mechanobiology and metabolism.

title: First 3D Bioprinted Placenta Model for Study of Preeclampsia Created
excerpt: Scientists are the first to create a 3D bioprinted placenta model and use it to study preeclampsia, a life-threatening pregnancy complication.

title: Schardt Awarded UMD-NCI Cancer Research Training Fellowship
excerpt: BioE graduate student recognized for efforts to develop novel molecular engineering approach to breast and ovarian cancer therapy.
